drama_notes: Brittany Mahomes is the most-discussed NFL wife — every social media post becomes a news cycle.
Brother Jackson Mahomes is a TikToker who has had legal issues; quietly stays away from most games.
Andy Reid is the father figure — Patrick has talked openly about how much Reid shaped him.
After the 2024 Super Bowl parade shooting, Patrick was vocal about gun reform — a notable political moment for an NFL star.
headlines: Signed the largest contract in pro sports history in 2020 — 10 years, $450M. Now considered underpaid relative to peers.
Won 3 Super Bowls in 5 years. Tied with Brady's pace.
Faced his first 'down year' in 2024 — sparked early 'is the dynasty over' takes that aged terribly.
memes: The side-arm submarine pass through traffic is its own meme genre.
His high-pitched voice — fans call out 'WORLD CHAMPIONS!' impressions.
Every State Farm commercial spawns a new meme.
The 'I think the Chiefs are going to lose' jinx never works.
on_field_moments: Super Bowl LIV comeback against the 49ers (2020) — first ring.
OT Super Bowl LVIII win against the 49ers (2024).
The no-look pass that landed on the Madden cover.
off_field: Married to Brittany Matthews, his high-school sweetheart from Whitehouse, TX.
Two kids: Sterling Skye and Bronze.
Owns a stake in the Kansas City Royals (MLB) and Sporting KC (MLS).
Founded the 15 and the Mahomies Foundation for childhood literacy.
running_jokes: Andy Reid is his actual dad in everything but DNA.
The 'sliding into the playoffs at 6-5' is now a Chiefs tradition.
He's somehow a 30-year-old veteran QB who looks 22.

drama_notes: Dating Taylor Swift since summer 2023. Her presence at games drives 30%+ of NFL TV viewership.
Older brother Jason Kelce retired in 2024; their New Heights podcast is the most-listened-to NFL pod.
Got into a viral sideline screaming match with Andy Reid in Super Bowl LVIII; walked it back after.
Hosted SNL in 2023, did a Pfizer commercial — full crossover star.
headlines: Eras Tour cameos became the cultural moment of the decade.
Won his third Super Bowl in 2024.
Launched a movie production company in 2024.
memes: 'You belong with me' — Travis tossing his hat at the Eras Tour.
Andy Reid hugging Travis in a Super Bowl moment.
The 'New Heights' brothers podcast clips that go viral every week.
on_field_moments: Super Bowl LVII game-winning catch.
The 11-catch divisional round game vs. Buffalo.
Career receptions leader for tight ends — broke Tony Gonzalez's record.
off_field: From Cleveland Heights, OH — same family as Jason (Eagles legend).
Hosts New Heights podcast with Jason — top NFL podcast in America.
Lives in Kansas City — bought a $6M mansion that became Travis-and-Taylor headquarters.
running_jokes: His mustache. Everyone has takes on the mustache.
He's somehow always at every Met Gala / Super Bowl / Grammys now.
Jason crying at his retirement is now used in every meme about emotion.

drama_notes: Engaged to actress Hailee Steinfeld since 2024 — the wedding is one of the year's most-anticipated.
Old college tweets resurfaced in 2018; he addressed them publicly and apologized.
Famous cliff-jumping video from a vacation went viral; he loves the outdoors content.
Has lost to Mahomes 4 times in the playoffs — the heartbreak runs deep.
headlines: MVP of the 2024 season.
The 13-second collapse in the 2022 divisional round vs. KC will follow him forever.
Bills extended him through 2030 — generational money.
memes: Pretzel-throwing Bills fans for Josh after his cliff-jumping vacation pic.
The 'Bills Mafia' table-jumping for every big play.
Josh hurdling defenders is its own highlight genre.
on_field_moments: The 2022 divisional round vs. KC where the Bills lost in OT after never getting the ball.
The hurdle over the Saints defender in 2021.
His 4-TD playoff games are now an annual event.
off_field: From Firebaugh, CA — small town, his family runs a farm.
Has a dog named Reign.
Started dating Hailee Steinfeld in 2023; engaged 2024.
running_jokes: He throws fastballs at receivers' chests on routine 5-yard slants.
The 'we're so back' / 'it's so over' Bills fan cycle each Sunday.
Josh runs more than most RBs — and the Bills don't always like that.

drama_notes: Famously private. Has dated Bryonna 'Bry' Burrows — same school sweethearts.
Took the Eagles QB1 job from Carson Wentz in 2020; never looked back.
Quiet, methodical, refuses to do drama press conferences.
headlines: Won the Super Bowl in 2025 (LIX) over the Chiefs.
The Saquon Barkley signing made him untouchable.
Threw for 300+ yards in his first 5 starts of 2024.
memes: The Tush Push. 'Brotherly Shove.' Every short-yardage play, the Eagles run it.
His pre-game outfit drops are A.J. Brown-tier.
The 'Jalen reads books' photo (he reads on the team plane).
on_field_moments: Super Bowl LIX — outdueled Mahomes for his first ring.
The 4-TD performance in Super Bowl LVII (the loss).
The MNF dagger to A.J. Brown vs. the Cowboys.
off_field: From Houston, TX — went to Channelview HS.
Played at Alabama, transferred to Oklahoma, lost the Heisman to Joe Burrow.
Fitness routine is famously intense — extra stretches, extra reps after practice.
running_jokes: He plays like he's been here before — even when he hasn't.
The 'is Jalen even in the locker room?' jokes — he leaves before reporters get there.
The Tush Push is technically not banned. Yet.

drama_notes: Long-term partner Olivia Holzmacher has been with him since LSU days; very private.
Mint Cartiers walk at the 2022 AFC Championship became the most-discussed pregame fit ever.
Has been injured in 3 of his 5 NFL seasons — wrist, calf, appendix.
Went viral for saying he liked Drake — Bengals immediately Googled which Drake.
headlines: Came back from his 2023 wrist injury to put up huge 2024 numbers.
Threw for 5,200 yards in a season — most ever for a Bengal.
Lost Super Bowl LVI to the Rams in his second year.
memes: His pregame fits — the Fendi sunglasses, the black turtlenecks.
The 'I'm built for this shit' quote after the LSU national championship.
His house being burglarized while he was on the road in 2024.
on_field_moments: The 4th-and-20 pass to Tee Higgins in the 2021 playoffs vs. KC.
The 525-yard playoff game.
Coming back from an ACL tear in college to win the Heisman in his 5th year.
off_field: From The Plains, OH — football town through and through.
Father (Jim Burrow) was a college football coach.
Made it through Ohio State, transferred to LSU, won everything.
running_jokes: He's permanently in 'main character' mode.
The 'Joe Cool' nickname is doing all the work.
Cincinnati became the most fashionable city in football because of him.

drama_notes: Negotiated his own contract — no agent. Ravens signed him to a 5-year, $260M deal in 2023.
Mom Felicia Jones is his manager — central to all his decisions.
Has had three different OCs in three years; constantly tweaking the offense around him.
The 'will he run too much?' debate is now in its 8th year.
headlines: Two-time MVP (2019, 2023) — youngest unanimous MVP ever.
Has thrown for 4,000+ yards in a season — proved he's not 'just' a runner.
Lost the 2024 AFC Championship to KC at home.
memes: The 'man cannot be guarded' clips on every Sunday.
His draft slide (32nd overall) is now a permanent NFL embarrassment.
The 'bad weather game' meme — his stats in cold/wet weather are scrutinized every January.
on_field_moments: The 2019 MVP season where he threw for 36 TDs and ran for 1,200 yards.
The 71-yard run vs. the Bengals in 2019 where he juked 4 defenders.
Throwing 5 TDs vs. the Lions in 2023.
off_field: From Pompano Beach, FL — raised by mom Felicia after father died young.
Famously private — Instagram deactivated multiple times.
Has a son.
Owns a youth football camp in Florida.
running_jokes: Every January, Twitter rediscovers that he hasn't won a Super Bowl yet.
Felicia Jones doing color commentary on his contract talks would be must-watch TV.
He runs faster than RBs and throws like a QB — the cheat code.

drama_notes: Off-field history is well-documented; he has addressed it publicly multiple times.
The Miami arrest video before the 2024 season opener became a national story.
Threatened to retire mid-season 2024 over team direction; stayed.
headlines: Caught for 1,799 yards in 2023 — most in a season.
Forced his way out of KC in 2022; the trade reshaped both teams.
Engaged in public back-and-forth with Travis Kelce and Mahomes after the trade — all eventually friendly.
memes: The Cheetah hand sign after every big catch.
His race vs. an Olympic sprinter (he beat him in 60 meters).
Hurdling defenders at full speed — 4.29 forty.
The 'I'm a mama's boy' quote that always gets pulled out.
on_field_moments: The 75-yard TD vs. the Patriots in 2023.
The Olympic-level kickoff returns from his early KC days.
Catching everything Tua throws him for two straight seasons.
off_field: From Pearson, GA — small town, played multiple sports.
Had Olympic-level track times in college.
Has launched multiple businesses, including a sneaker collab.
Lives in Miami year-round.
running_jokes: Tua deep ball to Tyreek = guaranteed touchdown.
The 'We don't run the 40, we run the 60' line — track guys get it.

drama_notes: Highest-paid non-QB in NFL history (signed in 2024 — 4yr/$140M).
Held out of OTAs in 2023 over the contract — Vikings paid up.
Had to learn 4 different QBs throw him the ball in 5 years.
Had a hamstring injury in 2023 that limited him for half the season.
headlines: 1,800+ receiving yard seasons — most by a WR in a 4-year span.
Won Offensive Player of the Year in 2022.
Vikings have never won a playoff game with him — the Daunte Culpepper era references write themselves.
memes: The Griddy. He owns the Griddy. Period.
His one-handed catch vs. the Bills in 2022 — replays it on Twitter every week.
JJ + Sam Darnold (his 2024 QB) became unexpectedly fun.
on_field_moments: The one-handed catch vs. Buffalo in 2022 (4th and 18, season-saver).
The 4-TD game vs. Detroit in 2023.
His 1,800-yard season.
off_field: From St. Rose, LA — older brother Jordan also played in the NFL.
LSU national championship team alongside Burrow and Chase.
Loves video games — streamed Madden.
running_jokes: The 'Vikings are about to ruin Justin Jefferson' is an annual offseason take.
He invented the Griddy. Carolina Panthers RB Chuba Hubbard wishes he had.
When Sam Darnold of all people had a great year throwing to him, the universe made sense.

drama_notes: Giants offered him 'less than the league minimum' to sign in 2024 (per his own quotes); he walked.
Eagles signed him; he immediately ran for 2,000 yards and won a Super Bowl.
His Twitter clap-back at Giants ownership after every Eagles win is its own genre.
Became the face of 'pay your stars' in CBA conversations.
headlines: Ran for 2,005 yards in 2024 — second most ever.
Won Super Bowl LIX in his first Eagles season.
Penn State legend turned NFL legend — finally healthy.
memes: The hurdle vs. the Jaguars in 2024 — vertical leap that defied physics.
Every time the Giants lose, Eagles fans tweet 'Saquon Hurdle' GIFs at them.
Daniel Jones eating a burger while Saquon hurdled — never recovers.
on_field_moments: The reverse hurdle vs. Jacksonville in 2024.
2,005-yard regular season.
The Super Bowl LIX clinch run.
off_field: From the Bronx, NY — raised in Pennsylvania.
Father Alibay Barkley was a champion boxer.
Has a daughter, Jada.
Notable Penn State alum — was the Heisman runner-up in 2017.
running_jokes: Every Eagles fan owes him a beer for life.
The Giants front office holds a moment of silence every Sunday.
His hurdles need their own Pro Bowl skills competition.

drama_notes: Painted nails became national news — 'is the Bears QB too pretty?' became a stupid take in 2024.
Cried with his dad on draft night — emotional Manning-style moment.
Father Carl is heavily involved in his career; before the draft, threatened the Bears wouldn't get him.
Threw for record-low TDs as a rookie behind a terrible OL — Bears fired the OC.
Hired Ben Johnson as new HC for 2025; the messianic optimism returns.
headlines: USC Heisman winner. #1 overall pick of the 2024 NFL Draft.
Rookie year was rough — Bears went 5-12.
Ben Johnson hire (former Lions OC) is the most-anticipated coach-QB pairing in football.
memes: The painted nails — Carlos Mendoza commercial era.
Bears QB graveyard — 'Mr. Williams, here's your headstone' joke per Bears Twitter.
His 'mom tweets' from his actual mom going viral.
on_field_moments: The Hail Mary vs. Washington as a rookie.
The 4-TD game vs. the Vikings.
His behind-the-back pass at USC (still plays on highlight reels).
off_field: From Washington, D.C. — went to Gonzaga College HS.
Father Carl is a real estate guy who managed his NIL deals.
Has appeared in commercials for Dr. Pepper, Wendy's.
running_jokes: The Chicago Bears are now a brand-management agency for him.
Every Bears fan is in their 'this time it's different' arc.
The painted nails will outlive the Caleb hate.

drama_notes: Got the biggest contract in NFL history (briefly) in 2024 — 4 years, $240M.
Hasn't won a playoff game since 2018.
Mom Peggy died of colon cancer in 2013; he speaks about her constantly.
Brother Jace died by suicide in 2020; Dak became a mental-health advocate.
headlines: Threw for 4,500+ yards in 2023.
Has been the Cowboys' QB since 2016 — Tony Romo's heir.
Coach Mike McCarthy fired in 2024; new HC era begins.
memes: Jerry Jones publicly toying with replacing him every offseason.
The 12-5 / one-and-done playoff loop.
His 'Faith Through It All' tattoo references his family losses.
on_field_moments: The 2016 rookie year (13-3, lost to Aaron Rodgers in playoffs).
Threw 6 TDs in a single game vs. the Eagles in 2017.
Came back from a fractured ankle in 2020.
off_field: From Sulphur, LA. Mississippi State alum.
Brothers Tad and Jace shaped his life.
Mental-health advocacy work after his brother's death.
running_jokes: January Dak vs. November Dak is a different person.
Jerry's annual Dak press conference is must-watch comedy.
Cowboys fans go through 5 stages of grief every December.

drama_notes: Cousin of Nickeil Alexander-Walker (Wolves) — they grew up together.
Quietly became the most-respected player in the league among players themselves.
Has refused to do typical media — postgame outfits do the talking.
Engaged to Hailey Summers; very private.
headlines: Reigning NBA MVP (2024).
Led the Thunder to the #1 seed in the West with the youngest roster in the league.
First All-NBA team in 2023; never looked back.
memes: The cardigans and tailored fits postgame.
His two-word answers in postgame interviews.
OKC fans calling him 'SGA' like a rapper name.
on_court_moments: The 50-point game vs. Charlotte in 2023.
The 38-point Game 7 win over Denver in 2024.
His step-back into the mid-range (a literal trademark).
off_field: From Hamilton, Ontario, Canada.
Father played pro basketball in Antigua.
Has a fashion brand and is a guest at fashion week regularly.
Moved from the Clippers (drafted) to OKC in 2019 — lottery for the Thunder.
running_jokes: The 'foul' celebration where he just stares at the ref.
He's the youngest 30-year-old in the league.
Says nothing in postgame, says everything in his fits.

drama_notes: Loves his horses more than basketball — has said this on record multiple times.
Won 2023 NBA Championship and went home to Sombor — didn't even go to the parade for long.
Three MVPs (2021, 2022, 2024) — most for a center in modern era.
Brothers Strahinja and Nemanja are constantly courtside; family is very close.
headlines: Three-time NBA MVP.
Won the 2023 NBA Finals MVP.
Has a perfectly average athletic profile and is the best player in the world.
memes: Jokic 'meh face' — the most expressive shrugs in basketball.
His passes that throw the ball through three defenders.
The 'Big Honey' nickname.
Standing perfectly still and getting 30-15-10.
on_court_moments: The 2023 Game 5 Finals clinch over Miami.
His 41-15-10 in Game 4 of the 2024 conference semis.
The wrap-around pass to Aaron Gordon for a poster dunk.
off_field: From Sombor, Serbia.
Owns multiple racehorses — talks about them in interviews.
Wife Natalija — high-school sweetheart.
Has a daughter named Ognjena.
running_jokes: He'd retire tomorrow and go to the racetrack and we'd all understand.
Coach Malone losing his mind on the sideline while Jokic shrugs.

drama_notes: @gethigher77 burner accounts — 9 years of secret Twitter activity targeting his own teammates.
Famously thin-skinned online; replies to randoms in his DMs.
Beefed publicly with Devin Booker, Frank Vogel, Jabari Smith Jr — all teammates.
Once tweeted defending himself in the third person from his own account.
Traded from Phoenix to Houston in 2025 — one of the biggest trades in modern history.
headlines: Two-time NBA Champion (2017, 2018) and Finals MVP both years.
Most skilled scorer in NBA history.
First star to publicly use a 'mental health day' (2018).
memes: The burner account saga — 'KD wrote that' is now an evergreen meme.
His 'I'm not here to get into Twitter nonsense' quote when asked about the burner.
Comparing his teammates to dictators in burner messages.
on_court_moments: The 2017 Finals game-winning 3 over LeBron.
Game 5, 2019 Finals — Achilles tear in Toronto.
The 50-point closeout vs. the Bucks in 2021 (with Kyrie hurt).
off_field: From Suitland, MD; raised by mom Wanda — 'The Real MVP' speech is legendary.
Investments in Boardroom (his media company), Coinbase, Postmates.
Founded Thirty Five Ventures — his media + investment firm.
running_jokes: He has a burner. Always. We've moved past asking.
The 'narrating his own teammates from the shadows' gag never gets old.

drama_notes: Plays alongside his son Bronny — first father-son NBA duo to share the court.
His agency Klutch Sports (run by Rich Paul) basically runs the Lakers.
Calls his shots months in advance — saying he wants to play with Bronny was a years-long campaign.
Has feuded with everyone from Skip Bayless to Stephen A. Smith on social.
Famously can-cancel anyone — Carmelo, Anthony Davis, Russ — through Klutch's leverage.
headlines: All-time NBA scoring leader (passed Kareem in 2023).
Four-time NBA Champion.
Plays his son Bronny on the same NBA team — historic moment.
memes: The Decision (2010) — still a meme 15 years later.
Crying LeBron (the 2018 Finals collapse).
Crowning himself in his Cleveland tunnel.
Every 'this is his last year' annual prediction since 2018.
on_court_moments: The 2016 chase-down block on Andre Iguodala (Game 7 Finals).
The 2020 NBA Championship in the bubble.
Passing Kareem in 2023 — emotional family moment.
off_field: From Akron, OH — single-mom upbringing famously chronicled.
Wife Savannah, three kids: Bronny, Bryce, Zhuri.
Founded I PROMISE School in Akron — accredited public school for at-risk kids.
Owns a piece of Liverpool FC, Pittsburgh Penguins, Boston Red Sox.
running_jokes: The 'last dance' announcement happens every year for 8 years now.
He's been in the Finals 10 times — he counts the appearances differently than the wins.
The Klutch Lakers running joke — 'you mean Klutch Sports?'

drama_notes: 7'4 with the wingspan of an albatross. The body is a national French project — they monitored him from age 14.
Famously shy and curious — collects books, plays chess.
France's basketball federation made huge mistakes with him as a teenager (overplaying him); the Spurs are protecting him at all costs.
Pop is 76 and is essentially babysitting him until he becomes the face of the league.
headlines: Defensive Player of the Year favorite, every year.
Most-hyped rookie since LeBron.
Took the Spurs from worst to play-in contention in two seasons.
memes: His face when he blocks shots — the 'oh' moment.
Sad Wemby on the bench (Spurs losing) becoming a meme.
His French interviews where he just says profound stuff in his second language.
on_court_moments: The 5x5 game vs. Phoenix in his rookie year — points/rebounds/assists/steals/blocks.
The buzzer-beater 3 vs. the Lakers.
The 2024 Olympic gold medal game vs. Team USA where he hung 26 on Embiid.
off_field: From Le Chesnay, France — outside Paris.
Mother Elodie was a basketball coach.
Reads philosophy in his free time — referenced Zarathustra in an interview.
Lives in San Antonio with his family who relocated.
running_jokes: He's gentle. He's terrifying. He's the future.
Pop wraps him in bubble wrap nightly.

drama_notes: Ant Man. The marketing future of the league.
Mom and grandma both died when he was 14 — raised by his sister Antoinette since.
The 'I'm the best player in the world' interview at age 23 was bold and earned.
Reportedly has multiple kids with multiple women; a paternity story broke in 2024.
Mike Conley is his locker-room dad.
headlines: Made first All-NBA team in 2024.
Took the Wolves to the Western Conference Finals in 2024.
Olympic gold medalist (Paris 2024).
memes: The 'It's me. It's just me.' interview.
Air-walk dunks on every poster in the league.
His 'Bring it Sportscenter!' celebration.
His high-pitched laugh that everyone has imitated.
on_court_moments: The 40-point Game 7 closeout vs. Denver in 2024.
The poster on John Collins (a literal poster).
The Olympic gold-medal-game 3 to crush France.
off_field: From Atlanta, GA — Holy Spirit Prep then Georgia.
Sister Antoinette is his guardian-mother figure.
Recently got into golf — surprisingly good.
running_jokes: He says everything Mike Conley wishes he could.
He laughs at his own jokes for a full 45 seconds postgame.
He thinks he could've been an NFL safety. Honestly, maybe.

drama_notes: Has a son with ex Toriah Lachell — co-parent with him courtside often.
Quietly the most-targeted star by NBA Twitter — relentless 'is he a #1 option?' takes.
Famously close with Kobe Bryant before Kobe's death; talks about him often.
headlines: NBA Champion in 2024 — first All-NBA #3 pick to win one.
The 6th-most playoff points by age 25 in history.
Made the Finals in 2022, lost to Steph Curry.
memes: Deuce Tatum (his son) being held up after every championship moment.
The 'it's a 3' celebration mid-shot.
His 'Kobe taught me' answers in every interview.
on_court_moments: The 51-point Game 7 vs. the 76ers in 2023.
The 31-point title clincher in 2024.
His rookie poster on LeBron in the 2018 ECF.
off_field: From St. Louis, MO — Chaminade Prep.
Played one year at Duke under Coach K.
Has a son Deuce (born 2017) with Toriah.
running_jokes: He's somehow always 'underrated' despite being a top-3 player.
Deuce is the actual MVP at every Celtics championship event.
The 'why is JT so calm?' clips after big shots.

drama_notes: TRADED to the Lakers in 2025 in the most shocking deal in NBA history. Mavericks fans organized a march.
Has been called out by media for conditioning multiple times.
Beefs with refs every game — yells in Slovenian.
Engaged to Anamaria Goltes; they have a daughter Gabriela.
headlines: Took the Mavericks to the 2024 NBA Finals (lost to Boston).
Traded to the Lakers in February 2025 in a deal that changed both franchises forever.
EuroLeague championship and EuroBasket gold medal — both at age 19.
memes: His step-back 3 — the most-replicated move at YMCAs everywhere.
The 'Luka complains to the ref every play' compilation.
His face when he scores — the 'this is too easy' look.
on_court_moments: The 73-point game vs. the Hawks in 2024.
The Game 7 stepback dagger over Phoenix in 2022.
The 41-point game in Game 1 of the 2024 NBA Finals.
off_field: From Ljubljana, Slovenia. Played pro at age 16.
Engaged to Anamaria Goltes since 2023; daughter Gabriela born 2023.
running_jokes: He plays at his own pace. The pace of someone who knows he's the smartest guy on the court.
The 'Luka magic' is just basketball IQ played in slow motion.

drama_notes: Wife Ayesha is a media mogul — restaurants, cookbooks, kids' brands. Ayesha + Steph as a brand is bigger than the Currys themselves.
Father Dell played in the NBA; mother Sonya is a former volleyball player. Both are in the news constantly.
Brother Seth plays for the Hornets; family games are wholesome content.
Tagged as 'the nicest guy in the league' — maintained that for 16 years.
headlines: Four-time NBA Champion (2015, 2017, 2018, 2022).
First unanimous MVP in NBA history (2016).
All-time NBA 3-point leader by a country mile.
memes: The 'night-night' celebration after dagger 3s.
Mouthpiece chewing while he runs back on D.
Steph dancing on the bench during garbage time.
His pregame tunnel routine — every dribble is choreographed.
on_court_moments: The 2022 NBA Finals MVP (his first).
The 60-point game vs. the Hawks in 2021.
The unanimous MVP season in 2015-16 (73 wins).
off_field: From Charlotte, NC — Davidson College alum.
Wife Ayesha, three kids: Riley, Ryan, Canon.
Founded Eat. Learn. Play. — Bay Area youth nonprofit.
running_jokes: He invented the 3 and we owe him an honorary degree.
Ayesha is the most-discussed NBA spouse on the internet.
Klay being his brother in everything but DNA.

drama_notes: Bell's palsy diagnosis affected him in 2024-25 — talked openly about it.
Brother died in 2014; Joel still references the loss in interviews.
Switched eligibility from Cameroon to USA basketball — French fans booed him at the 2024 Olympics.
headlines: MVP, 2023 — first big-man MVP in 23 years.
Played 39 games in 2024-25 due to Bell's palsy and knee surgery.
Olympic silver medalist (Paris 2024) with Team USA.
memes: 'Trust the Process' chants from Sixers fans, dating back to 2014.
The 'I am the bus driver' postgame quote.
His 'shimmy' celebration after big buckets.
on_court_moments: The 70-point game vs. the Spurs in 2024 (76ers franchise record).
The 50-point game vs. the Kings with one eye swollen shut.
The 2023 ECF buzzer-beater dagger over Tatum.
off_field: From Yaoundé, Cameroon — moved to the US at 16, started playing basketball at 15.
Wife Anne de Paula is a Brazilian model; son Arthur born in 2020.
Speaks 4 languages: French, English, Cameroonian Pidgin, some Spanish.
running_jokes: The 'Trust the Process' chant has outlived three Sixers GMs.
He's a 7-footer who has the comedic timing of a stand-up.

drama_notes: His three brothers also played pro basketball — Thanasis is on the Bucks with him.
Stayed loyal to Milwaukee with a supermax extension when LA + NYC were calling.
Damian Lillard pairing has not produced playoff success — public 'is the window closed?' takes.
Faced down a reporter who suggested 'failure' after a playoff loss — the 'no failure, only steps' speech is iconic.
headlines: Two-time NBA MVP (2019, 2020).
NBA Champion (2021) and Finals MVP — the 50-point closeout is canon.
Olympic medalist with Greece in Paris 2024.
memes: The 'no failure, only steps' speech becomes a motivational meme every season.
His footwork dribbling — every Eurostep clip.
The Greek Freak / 'this man is a freak of nature' announcer reactions.
on_court_moments: The 50-point Game 6 close-out of the 2021 NBA Finals.
The Eastern Conference Finals comeback vs. Hawks (2021).
The 64-point game vs. the Pacers in 2023.
off_field: From Athens, Greece — parents Nigerian immigrants.
Partner Mariah Riddlesprigger, three kids: Liam, Maverick, Eva.
Founded the AntetokounBros Academy in Greece for youth basketball.
running_jokes: He'll one-handed dunk over you and then ask if you're okay.
He gets paid $50M to be the most positive person in any room.

drama_notes: Two suspensions in 18 months for gun-related Instagram videos. The second nearly cost him a season.
Was at the center of multiple lawsuits and incidents — a teenager was punched at his house in 2022.
Came back from each suspension in dominant fashion — the 'comeback kid' narrative is real.
Father Tee Morant is courtside every game — a fan favorite.
headlines: Most Improved Player, 2022.
Took the Grizzlies to the #2 seed in 2022.
Suspended 25 games at start of 2023-24 season.
memes: 'JA TIME!' celebrations after every poster.
His pregame 'I'm him' attitude — and the postgame backlash when he loses.
Father Tee Morant courtside trash-talking is its own content stream.
on_court_moments: The 47-point Game 5 vs. the Warriors in 2022.
The Mike Conley alley-oop close-out vs. the Lakers (2024).
His rookie-year poster on Aron Baynes.
off_field: From Dalzell, SC.
Father Tee was a college basketball player.
Best friends with Tyus Jones and Desmond Bane.
running_jokes: Pre-game Ja and post-suspension Ja are different people.
The Memphis fan base is loyal to a fault — they ride for him.

drama_notes: Demanded out of Miami in early 2025; was traded to Golden State.
Burned bridges in Chicago, Minnesota, Philly — all on his way to Miami.
Big Face Coffee — runs a coffee company. Charged Heat teammates $20 a cup.
Plays harder in the playoffs than the regular season; the 'Playoff Jimmy' meme is empirically true.
His relationship with Erik Spoelstra in Miami was the league's best — and ended over a public spat.
headlines: Six-time All-Star, two-time Finals appearances.
Took the Heat to the Finals in 2020 and 2023.
Traded to Golden State at 2025 deadline.
memes: 'Big Face Coffee' — actually a real brand.
The Disney bubble photo where he's reading a newspaper.
His emo phase (face piercings, painted nails) in 2023.
The 'Play-In Jimmy beats you in the regular season' meme.

The 47-point Game 7 vs. the Bucks in 2022.
off_field: From Houston, TX — adopted by a family at 13 after being kicked out of his mother's home at 13.
Big Face Coffee is a real, successful business.
Famously drinks coffee like it's Gatorade.
running_jokes: Playoff Jimmy is a different player than Regular-Season Jimmy.
Whoever he's mad at this week, you'll know within 24 hours.
The Big Face Coffee logo is on more shirts than Heat jerseys in Miami.

drama_notes: Forced his way out of Portland in 2023 — 'Dame Time' in Milwaukee.
Marriage to Kay'la Hanson ended in a public, ugly divorce in 2023; Dame talked about it openly.
Engaged in 2025 to Bria Bartholomew.
Suffered Achilles tear in 2024 playoffs; recovery is a major storyline.
Damian + Giannis pairing has not produced playoff success — both have voiced frustration.
headlines: 8-time All-Star.

running_jokes: If the game is on the line, give it to Dame.

The wave goodbye should be the league's logo.

drama_notes: The face of the Pistons rebuild — went 14-68 his rookie year, came back to be an All-Star.
Cade is not the loudest superstar — quiet leadership has won over the locker room.

drama_notes: Famously private — has dated the same person, Madison Beckford, since college.

drama_notes: Concussion saga — three documented concussions, league protocol changes named after him.
